entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A staple crop in West Africa, specifically the Guinea-race, targeted for productivity improvement

entities.name: WEST AFRICA
entities.description: The geographical region where the research is focused and where Guinea-race sorghum is indigenous

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: A societal goal aimed to be improved by increasing the productivity of sorghum

entities.name: FARMER'S INCOMES
entities.description: An economic goal aimed to be increased by improving sorghum productivity

entities.name: ADAPTATION
entities.description: The ability of Guinea-race sorghum to thrive in West African environments

entities.name: GRAIN QUALITY
entities.description: A desirable trait of Guinea-race sorghum

entities.name: GENETIC POTENTIAL
entities.description: The latent capacity of West African sorghums to be unlocked for better performance

entities.name: GUINEA-RACE
entities.description: A specific race of indigenous sorghum found in West Africa known for adaptation and grain quality

entities.name: YIELD STABILITY
entities.description: The ability of Guinea-race sorghum to maintain consistent yields

entities.name: YIELD LEVELS
entities.description: The quantitative measure of sorghum production, which rarely exceeds 2 tons/ha in farmer's fields

entities.name: HYBRID TECHNOLOGY
entities.description: A method used to improve the productivity and benefits of staple crops like sorghum
